description_long: This bus shelter features a sturdy, arched metal roof with visible weathering and rust, supported by a blue and gray metal frame. The shelter has three large back panels and partial side panels, providing protection from wind and rain while remaining open at the front for easy access. Integrated into the structure is a small kiosk or information panel on one end, and two metal bike racks are positioned in front of the shelter. The shelter's design is utilitarian, with exposed bolts and structural supports, and the materials show signs of outdoor exposure. The color palette is dominated by muted blue, gray, and brown tones, giving it an industrial, urban appearance. The shelter is large enough to accommodate several people and offers a covered waiting area for public transportation users.
description: A weathered, arched-roof bus shelter with blue and gray metal panels, partial side enclosures, and integrated bike racks in front.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved roof, end panel, and the attached kiosk or information panel.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the open entry, three back panels, partial side panels, and two bike racks in front.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the arched roof, bike racks, and the absence of a kiosk on this end.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the full length of the back panels and the shelter's structural supports.
